text.title: User intent to phase mapping
text.summary: Maps the user's invocation phrasing (Ukrainian or English) to the minimal phase set the orchestrator should run. Phase 0 is implicit in every row except pure Commit (Phase 6) and Skill Creation (Phase 7), which act on already-captured state.

list.item: (no args) or "що ми зробили?" or "what did we learn?" → Phase 0 only, because session capture is the entire ask.
"audit X" or "find issues" or "audit my server" → Phase 0 + Phase 1, because the user wants a system scan on top of session capture.
"what can be improved?" → Phase 0 + Phase 1 + Phase 2, because the user wants both findings and prioritization.
"brainstorm improvements" → Phase 0 + Phase 3, because the user wants diverge-converge ideation, not a system scan.
"fix the issues" → Phase 3.5 + Phase 4, because findings already exist and the user is approving apply, but Phase 3.5 still runs to confirm specific options.
"log what we did" → Phase 0 + Phase 5, because the user wants the session captured and then written to the dated log.
"commit improvements" → Phase 6 only, because changes already exist on disk and the user just wants them committed.
"make a skill from this" → Phase 7 only, because the reusable-knowledge bar is being asserted and Phase 0 has already captured the underlying patterns.
"full improvement cycle" or "full audit and fix" → all phases (0 through 7), because the user explicitly opted into the complete workflow.

rule.statement: The orchestrator MUST select the minimal phase set consistent with the user's phrasing. When intent is ambiguous, ask via `AskUserQuestion` with 2-3 candidate phase sets — never guess upward into Phase 4 or Phase 6.
rule.rationale: Over-running phases burns tokens and risks unauthorized writes. Under-running is recoverable (the user can re-invoke); over-running is not.

rule.statement: Phase 3.5 MUST run before Phase 4 in every flow that includes Phase 4 — including the "fix the issues" shortcut and the "full improvement cycle" path.
rule.rationale: Phase 3.5 is the security boundary; no apply path is exempt. See `decisions.xml` for the rejected alternative.
